atoms.content: An ML Engineer focuses on building and scaling predictive models using traditional machine learning algorithms, while an AI Engineer often works with Large Language Models and generative AI APIs.
Think of an ML Engineer as a mechanic who builds custom engines from scratch, while an AI Engineer is someone who takes powerful, pre-built engines (like ChatGPT) and integrates them into cars.

1. ML Engineers train models from scratch on tabular or image data.
2. AI Engineers integrate existing foundation models (like GPT-4).
3. Both require strong software engineering skills.
In practice, these roles overlap significantly and vary by company. Many organizations merge them into a single 'ML/AI Engineer' position.
True or False: An ML Engineer and AI Engineer are completely separate roles with zero overlap. (Answer: False — they overlap significantly and many companies merge them).
